description: A designed 'GANG ARREST' rogues-gallery card built over a real arrest photograph. The base photo shows a detained Hispanic man in a white t-shirt and purple shorts standing handcuffed (hands behind back) between two heavily armed Salvadoran police officers in gray digital-camo tactical uniforms, body armor, and patrol caps; the officer at right cradles a rifle. Behind them is a wall bearing a blue-and-white 'POLICIA' (police) shield, indicating a Salvadoran police station. Across the image in large distressed cream/tan graffiti-style brush lettering are the words 'GANG' (top left, diagonal) and 'ARREST' (bottom right, diagonal), giving a gritty street/graffiti propaganda look. The top-right corner has a black banner reading 'MS-13 / La Mara Salvatrucha' in white serif type. A semi-transparent dark-red panel at lower left lists the subject in white sans-serif: 'Wilmer Alexy Garcia-Manzanarez' followed by bulleted charges: 'Wanted for Murder in El Salvador, Burglary, Menacing/intimidation with a weapon, DUI, Driving without a license.' The white DHS seal and 'ICE' wordmark sit at the bottom right. Propaganda technique: associates a named immigrant with the MS-13 'transnational terrorist' brand and a violent menu of crimes, using a perp-walk image and aggressive graffiti typography to dramatize a deportation as a public-safety victory. Target audience: the general public and ICE's social-media followers, reinforcing the administration's narrative that deportations remove dangerous gang members.
notable_text: MS-13 La Mara Salvatrucha; GANG ARREST; Wilmer Alexy Garcia-Manzanarez; Wanted for Murder in El Salvador; Burglary; Menacing/intimidation with a weapon; DUI; Driving without a license; ICE
